very_drunk.speaker: Sharon
very_drunk.text: Oh honey. You need to sit DOWN. I charge extra for therapy, and you look like you need the whole package.

buzzed.speaker: Sharon
buzzed.text: You're having a DAY, huh? Want to talk about it? It's $50 either way.

too_hot.text: The phone booth is empty. Just a dangling receiver swinging in the breeze. Sharon knows when to disappear.

regular.speaker: Sharon
regular.text: 'Back so soon, khakis? You've already got my color on your collar. Might as well come back for round two.'

morning_intro.text: The phone booth is empty. Too early for that kind of business. Sharon isn't up yet.

late_afternoon_intro.speaker: Sharon
late_afternoon_intro.text: 'Last call, khakis. After 5, I've got MY OWN BBQ to go to. I have a whole life, you know.'

already_met_lipstick.speaker: Sharon
already_met_lipstick.text: 'Back already? I figured. Regulars get a loyalty discount... jk. $75.'

already_met_drunk.speaker: Sharon
already_met_drunk.text: 'Honey. Come back when you can see straight. I'm not a therapist.'

already_met_default.text: She's still here. Waves. 'Business meeting?' she asks with air quotes.

beats.speaker: Sharon
beats.text: She leans against the phone booth. The phone booth is older than cell phones.

beats.speaker: Sharon
beats.text: Her eyes find yours across two gas pumps. You are located.

beats.speaker: Sharon
beats.text: "Hey honey. You look lost. Want some company?"

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: "I'm, uh — pump three — gas — yep — gas." You drop the nozzle. Twice.

curious.text: She laughs. 'Sure you are. I'm Destiny. Well, my real name's Sharon, but Destiny has better branding.'

t1.text: You talk. Actually just talk. She's funnier than anyone at your office. This is somehow more dangerous than anything else today.

leave.text: You leave. Sharon-Destiny waves goodbye. 'Come back anytime, gas man.'

decline.text: 'Suit yourself, khakis.' She goes back to her phone.
